title: An Act To Support and Improve the Maine Solid Waste Management Hierarchy and Save Taxpayer Money
ai_summary: First US packaging EPR law. Municipal reimbursement model â€” brands pay municipalities for recycling costs. Stewardship organization required.

covered_products: Packaging supplied with products sold in Maine
compliance_details.producer_definition: Brand owners and licensees of brands that supply covered materials in Maine
producer_obligations: Pay fees based on material type and weight

fees.details: Fee rates vary by material type and recyclability
fees.fee_notes: Rates TBD by PRO. Estimate based on comparable enacted packaging EPR programs.

exemptions: Small producers below annual revenue threshold

title: Plastic Pollution and Recycling Modernization Act
ai_summary: First operational US packaging EPR with active fees. Single PRO model. Subject to NAW constitutional challenge (trial July 13 2026). Producers must register and pay eco-modulated fees.

covered_products: Covered plastic and paper packaging distributed in Oregon
compliance_details.producer_definition: Brand owners or importers of covered packaging sold in Oregon with >$5M annual Oregon revenue
producer_obligations: Register with Circular Action Alliance PRO

fees.details: Rates vary by recyclability, recycled content, and material type. ~$0.04-$0.34/lb depending on material.
fees.fee_notes: Published rate ~$0.04-$0.34/lb (~$88-$750/tonne). Using midpoint ~$0.19/lb (~$419/tonne).

compliance_details.pro_requirements: Must join DEQ-approved PRO (currently Circular Action Alliance)
compliance_details.preemption_notes: NAW (National Association of Wholesaler-Distributors) filed suit challenging OR SB 582 under Dormant Commerce Clause. Trial set July 13 2026.

title: Plastic Pollution Reduction
ai_summary: Colorado packaging EPR. Colorado Circular Action Alliance (CAA) selected as PRO. Fees begin 2026. Covers all covered materials statewide.

covered_products: Covered materials including packaging and paper products
compliance_details.producer_definition: Brand owners and importers selling covered materials in Colorado

fees.details: Eco-modulated based on recyclability and recycled content
fees.fee_notes: Rates TBD by PRO. Estimate based on comparable enacted packaging EPR programs.

title: Plastic Pollution Prevention and Packaging Producer Responsibility Act
ai_summary: Largest US packaging EPR. ~$500M/yr producer fees. Requires 25% plastic packaging reduction by 2032, 65% recyclable/compostable by 2032. CalRecycle rulemaking still pending. Broadest covered material scope.

covered_products: All covered materials (packaging and paper products) sold in California
compliance_details.producer_definition: Brand owners, importers, and licensees of covered materials sold in California with >$1M annual California sales
producer_obligations: Annual supply data reporting by material type
Pay fees based on material weight and type

fees.details: ~$500M/yr total. Rates set by PRO based on CalRecycle-approved plan. Eco-modulation for recyclability, recycled content, toxicity.
fees.fee_notes: Aggregate ~$500M/yr target. Per-ton rate set by PRO. Estimate based on comparable programs.

exemptions: Certified B corporations meeting recycled content thresholds
compliance_details.pro_requirements: Must join CalRecycle-approved PRO or submit individual plan
compliance_details.reporting_requirements: Annual supply data report due March 31
compliance_details.preemption_notes: PACK Act introduced December 2025 would preempt state packaging labeling requirements. DOJ RFI August 2025 seeking state laws adversely affecting national economy.

title: Packaging Waste and Cost Reduction Act
ai_summary: Minnesota packaging EPR enacted 2024. Program planning underway. PRO selection process ongoing. Multiple PRO model allowed.

covered_products: Covered materials sold in Minnesota
compliance_details.producer_definition: Brand owners and importers of covered materials

fees.details: Eco-modulated fee structure, rates TBD by PRO
fees.fee_notes: Rates TBD by PRO. Estimate based on comparable enacted packaging EPR programs.

title: Extended Producer Responsibility for Packaging and Paper Products
ai_summary: Maryland packaging EPR. Allows multiple competing PROs â€” unique structural approach. MDE rulemaking underway.

covered_products: Covered materials sold in Maryland
compliance_details.producer_definition: Brand owners and importers meeting revenue thresholds

fees.details: Multiple PRO competition expected to drive efficiency
fees.fee_notes: Rates TBD by PRO. Estimate based on comparable enacted packaging EPR programs.

title: Reducing Plastic Packaging Pollution Through Producer Responsibility
ai_summary: Newest US packaging EPR (2025). Producer registration deadline December 2025. Ecology rulemaking to set fee rates.

covered_products: Covered packaging and paper products sold in Washington
compliance_details.producer_definition: Brand owners, importers, and licensees with >$1M WA annual revenue
producer_obligations: Register with Ecology-approved PRO by Dec 2025

fees.fee_notes: Rates TBD through Ecology rulemaking. Estimate based on comparable enacted packaging EPR programs.

title: Responsible Textile Producer Act
ai_summary: First US textile EPR law. Covers apparel and textile articles. PRO selection March 2026. California-only initially.

covered_products: Apparel and textile articles sold in California
compliance_details.producer_definition: Brand owners and retailers of apparel and textile articles

fees.details: Fee structure TBD by PRO and CalRecycle
fees.fee_notes: Fee structure TBD by PRO and CalRecycle. Estimate based on comparable textile EPR programs.

title: Battery Stewardship and Recycling Act of 2021
ai_summary: DC battery EPR. Covers primary and rechargeable batteries. Producers must join approved battery stewardship organization.

covered_products: All primary and rechargeable batteries sold in DC

title: Vermont Battery Stewardship Program
ai_summary: Vermont battery EPR. Call2Recycle as approved stewardship organization.

covered_products: Primary and rechargeable batteries
producer_obligations: Join Vermont ANR-approved battery stewardship program

title: Batteries: Recycling
ai_summary: California comprehensive battery EPR. Covers all battery types including EV batteries. CalRecycle oversight.

covered_products: All batteries sold in California including EV batteries

title: Solid Waste: Recyclability
ai_summary: California recyclability labeling. Products can only use recycling symbol if material is truly recyclable in 60%+ of CA jurisdictions. Eliminates aspirational recycling labels.

covered_products: All products bearing recyclability claims or symbols sold in California
producer_obligations: Remove recyclability symbols from non-recyclable packaging

deadlines.description: Full compliance with recyclability labeling rules

title: Solar Panels: End-of-Life
ai_summary: First US solar panel EPR. Manufacturers must provide take-back and recycling program for solar panels sold in Washington.

covered_products: Photovoltaic solar panels sold in Washington
producer_obligations: Provide free take-back and recycling program

title: PaintCare Stewardship Program â€” California
ai_summary: California paint stewardship via PaintCare. $0.35-$1.60 per container fee. Drop-off locations statewide.

covered_products: Architectural and industrial maintenance paint

fees.fee_notes: Using 1-5 gal tier ($0.95) as representative average. ~263 units/tonne (1-gal avg ~3.8kg).

title: PaintCare Stewardship Program â€” Oregon
ai_summary: Oregon paint stewardship via PaintCare. One of earliest US paint EPR programs.

covered_products: Architectural and industrial maintenance paint sold in Oregon

fees.fee_notes: Using 1-5 gal tier ($0.95) as representative average.

title: PaintCare Stewardship Program â€” Connecticut
ai_summary: Connecticut paint stewardship via PaintCare.

covered_products: Architectural and industrial maintenance paint sold in Connecticut

fees.fee_notes: Using 1-5 gal tier ($0.95) as representative average.

title: PaintCare Stewardship Program â€” Minnesota
ai_summary: Minnesota paint stewardship via PaintCare.

covered_products: Architectural and industrial maintenance paint sold in Minnesota

fees.fee_notes: Using 1-5 gal tier ($0.95) as representative average.

title: Mattress Recycling Program â€” California
ai_summary: California mattress EPR. Bye Bye Mattress / MRC stewardship program. $16-26 eco-fee per mattress at retail.

covered_products: Mattresses and box springs sold in California
producer_obligations: Join Mattress Recycling Council (MRC) stewardship program
Display eco-fee at point of sale

fees.fee_notes: Using queen size ($19) as representative average. Avg mattress ~25kg = 40/tonne.

title: Mattress Stewardship Program â€” Connecticut
ai_summary: Connecticut mattress EPR via MRC/Bye Bye Mattress.

covered_products: Mattresses sold in Connecticut

fees.fee_notes: Using queen size ($19) as representative average.

title: Mattress Stewardship Program â€” Rhode Island
ai_summary: Rhode Island mattress EPR via MRC.

covered_products: Mattresses sold in Rhode Island

fees.fee_notes: Using queen size ($19) as representative average.

title: Carpet Stewardship Program â€” California
ai_summary: California carpet EPR via CARE (Carpet America Recovery Effort). $0.25/sq yd assessment at point of sale.

covered_products: Carpet and rugs sold in California

fees.fee_notes: $0.25/sq yd. ~1.4kg/sq yd = ~714 sq yd/tonne.

title: Drug Take-Back Program â€” California
ai_summary: California pharmaceutical EPR. Drug manufacturers must fund year-round drug take-back. Also covers sharps.

covered_products: Covered drugs and sharps sold in California
compliance_details.producer_definition: Drug manufacturers selling covered drugs in California

title: Secure Drug Take-Back Act â€” Washington
ai_summary: Washington state pharmaceutical take-back EPR. Manufacturers fund collection.

covered_products: Covered drugs sold in Washington
producer_obligations: Fund and operate secure drug take-back program

title: Electronic Waste Recycling Act â€” California
ai_summary: California e-waste EPR. Covered electronic devices include TVs, monitors, laptops. Advance recovery fee at point of sale.

covered_products: Covered electronic devices (TVs, monitors, laptops, tablets)
producer_obligations: Covered Electronic Waste Recycling Fee applies at retail

fees.fee_notes: Using $14 midpoint of $8-$25 range. ~2kg avg device = 500 units/tonne.

title: Electronic Equipment Recycling and Reuse Act â€” New York
ai_summary: New York e-waste EPR. Manufacturer take-back program for covered electronics. Includes TVs, computers, peripherals, small electronics.

covered_products: Covered electronic equipment sold in New York

fees.fee_notes: Benchmark based on CA AB 1268 comparable program.

title: E-Cycle Washington â€” Electronic Stewardship Program
ai_summary: Washington state e-waste EPR. Free drop-off program for computers, monitors, TVs. Manufacturer-funded.

covered_products: Computers, monitors, and televisions

fees.fee_notes: Benchmark based on CA AB 1268 comparable program.

title: E-Cycles Connecticut â€” Electronic Recycling Program
ai_summary: Connecticut e-waste EPR. Manufacturer-funded free drop-off for computers, monitors, printers, TVs.

covered_products: Computers, monitors, printers, and televisions

fees.fee_notes: Benchmark based on CA AB 1268 comparable program.

title: Illinois Computer and Electronic Product Recycling Act
ai_summary: Illinois e-waste EPR. Covers computers, monitors, printers, and TVs. Manufacturer take-back and recycling obligation.

covered_products: Computers, monitors, printers, and televisions sold in Illinois

fees.fee_notes: Benchmark based on CA AB 1268 comparable program.

title: Minnesota Electronics Recycling Act
ai_summary: Minnesota e-waste EPR. Manufacturer-funded free collection for computers, TVs, and small electronics.

covered_products: Computers, monitors, TVs, and small electronics

fees.fee_notes: Benchmark based on CA AB 1268 comparable program.

title: E-Cycles Oregon â€” Electronic Stewardship Program
ai_summary: Oregon e-waste EPR. Covers computers, monitors, and televisions. Manufacturer-funded free drop-off.

covered_products: Computers, monitors, and televisions

fees.fee_notes: Benchmark based on CA AB 1268 comparable program.

title: New Jersey Electronic Waste Recycling Act
ai_summary: New Jersey e-waste EPR. Covers computers, monitors, TVs, and peripherals. Manufacturer take-back obligation.

covered_products: Computers, monitors, televisions, and peripherals

fees.fee_notes: Benchmark based on CA AB 1268 comparable program.

title: Massachusetts Electronic Device Stewardship Program
ai_summary: Massachusetts e-waste EPR. Covers televisions and computers. Manufacturer-funded collection program.

covered_products: Televisions and computers sold in Massachusetts

fees.fee_notes: Benchmark based on CA AB 1268 comparable program.

title: Illinois Battery Stewardship Act
ai_summary: Illinois battery EPR. Covers primary and rechargeable batteries. Producers must join approved battery stewardship organization (Call2Recycle or equivalent).

covered_products: Primary and rechargeable batteries sold in Illinois

title: Maryland Battery Stewardship Program
ai_summary: Maryland battery EPR. Part of Call2Recycle national network. Producers must join approved battery stewardship program.

covered_products: Primary and rechargeable batteries sold in Maryland

title: Washington Battery Stewardship Program
ai_summary: Washington state battery EPR. Covers primary and rechargeable batteries. Producers join Ecology-approved stewardship program.

covered_products: Primary and rechargeable batteries sold in Washington

title: New Jersey Battery Stewardship Act
ai_summary: New Jersey battery EPR. Covers primary and rechargeable batteries. Part of expanding northeast battery stewardship network.

covered_products: Primary and rechargeable batteries sold in New Jersey

ai_summary: Colorado paint stewardship via PaintCare. Drop-off locations statewide.

covered_products: Architectural and industrial maintenance paint sold in Colorado

fees.fee_notes: Using 1-5 gal tier ($0.95) as representative average.

ai_summary: Maine paint stewardship via PaintCare.

ai_summary: Vermont paint stewardship via PaintCare.

ai_summary: Washington state paint stewardship via PaintCare.

ai_summary: DC paint stewardship via PaintCare.

ai_summary: Oregon mattress EPR via MRC/Bye Bye Mattress.

covered_products: Mattresses and box springs sold in Oregon
producer_obligations: Join Mattress Recycling Council stewardship program
Display eco-fee at point of sale

title: New York Drug Take-Back Program
ai_summary: New York pharmaceutical take-back EPR. Drug manufacturers fund collection sites for unused medications.

covered_products: Covered drugs sold in New York
producer_obligations: Fund and operate drug take-back collection program

title: Massachusetts Drug Take-Back Program
ai_summary: Massachusetts pharmaceutical take-back EPR. Manufacturer-funded drug disposal program.

covered_products: Covered drugs sold in Massachusetts
producer_obligations: Fund and operate drug take-back collection program

title: Right to Repair Agricultural Equipment â€” Colorado
ai_summary: Colorado right-to-repair for agricultural equipment. Manufacturers must provide repair manuals, parts, and diagnostic tools to independent repair shops and owners.

covered_products: Agricultural equipment with embedded electronics
compliance_details.producer_definition: Manufacturers of agricultural equipment with embedded digital electronics
producer_obligations: Provide repair documentation to independent shops
Supply replacement parts at fair price

title: Digital Fair Repair Act â€” Minnesota
ai_summary: Minnesota Digital Fair Repair Act â€” broadest right-to-repair law enacted to date. Covers all digital electronic products. Manufacturers must provide repair documentation, parts, and tools to consumers and independent repair shops.

covered_products: All digital electronic products sold in Minnesota
producer_obligations: Supply parts and tools at fair price
compliance_details.preemption_notes: Industry lobbying for federal preemption of state right-to-repair laws. No federal law enacted yet.

title: Right to Repair Act â€” California
ai_summary: California Right to Repair Act. Covers electronics and appliances $50-$99.99 for 3 years, $100+ for 7 years. Manufacturers must provide parts, tools, and repair documentation.

covered_products: Electronics and appliances sold in California priced $50+
compliance_details.producer_definition: Manufacturers of covered electronics and appliances

deadlines.description: Manufacturers must provide repair access for covered products

enforcement.penalties: Civil penalties up to $1,000 per violation

compliance_details.preemption_notes: PACK Act-style federal bills could preempt state repair mandates. Currently no enacted federal right-to-repair.

title: Right to Repair Electronics â€” Oregon
ai_summary: Oregon right-to-repair for consumer electronics. Manufacturers must provide repair parts, tools, and documentation to owners and independent repair shops.

covered_products: Consumer electronic devices sold in Oregon
producer_obligations: Supply parts and tools at reasonable price

title: Plastic Packaging: Recycled Content Requirements â€” California
ai_summary: California plastic beverage container recycled content mandate. Requires 15% recycled content by 2022, 25% by 2025, 50% by 2030. Applies to plastic beverage containers.

covered_products: Plastic beverage containers sold in California
producer_obligations: Meet recycled content thresholds by deadline

fees.details: No fee â€” mandated minimum content threshold

title: Plastic Packaging Recycled Content and Recyclability â€” Washington
ai_summary: Washington plastic packaging recycled content and recyclability standards. Covers plastic packaging and plastic film. Requires minimum recycled content and recyclability standards.

covered_products: Plastic packaging and plastic film sold in Washington

deadlines.description: Initial recycled content and recyclability requirements

title: Michigan Bottle Deposit Law
ai_summary: Michigan bottle bill â€” highest deposit ($0.10) in the US. Covers beer, soft drinks, water, wine coolers, and juice. 97%+ return rate. Often cited as model for national deposit system.

covered_products: Carbonated and non-carbonated beverage containers

fees.details: $0.10 deposit per container â€” highest in the US
fees.fee_notes: Consumer-paid deposit system. Producer cost is administrative, not weight-based.

title: Oregon Bottle Bill â€” Expanded Deposit Return System
ai_summary: Oregon's landmark bottle bill â€” the first in the US (1971). Expanded in 2017 to include water and flavored water. $0.10 deposit. BottleDrop redemption network.

covered_products: Beer, carbonated soft drinks, water, and flavored water containers

fees.fee_notes: Consumer-paid deposit system. Producer cost is administrative, not weight-based.

title: California Beverage Container Recycling and Litter Reduction Act
ai_summary: California CRV (California Redemption Value) deposit system. Covers most beverage containers. $0.05 for <24 oz, $0.10 for 24 oz+. CalRecycle oversight.

covered_products: Most beverage containers sold in California

fees.details: $0.05 per container <24 oz, $0.10 per container 24 oz+
fees.fee_notes: Consumer-paid deposit system. Producer cost is administrative, not weight-based.
